outbound_official.notes: Official outbound page (for Kyrgyz citizens traveling abroad) last dated 19/01/2019 on the MFA site. Parent page: https://mfa.gov.kg/en/dks-mid-kr-en/Consular-Services/For-traveling-abroad/Visa-and-visa-free-regimes-for-citizens-of-the-Kyrgyz-Republic. Diplomatic and service passport holders have additional bilateral visa-free rights with 22+ countries (Hungary, India, Indonesia, Iran, South Korea, Kuwait, Pakistan, Romania, Serbia, Turkmenistan, Morocco, China, Chile, Brazil, Singapore, Laos, Lebanon, UAE, Bahrain, Jordan, Saudi Arabia, Japan - 30-90 days each). Diplomatic-only bilateral: Qatar, Italy, Poland, Estonia, Slovakia, Switzerland, France, Lithuania, Greece. The outbound page does not list e-visa or ETA for major destinations; those are destination-side data.

e_visa.notes: Since 2017, citizens of ALL countries and territories may apply for a Kyrgyzstan e-visa online via the official e-visa portal (evisa.e-gov.kg). Visa types include: Tourist (TS, up to 30 days), Business (B, up to 90 days), Investment (I, 5–10 years), Digital Nomad (DN, up to 1 year), Student (S, up to 90 days), Transit (TR, 3 days), Relatives (RL, up to 90 days), Driver (T, up to 6 months), Special Work SW1/SW2 (up to 60 days extendable to 1 year), Sapar (J, up to 1 year), Ethnic Kyrgyz 'Meken' (M, up to 1 year), and others. Fee depends on category; applications typically processed within 5 working days.

visa_policy.notes: Cabinet of Ministers Resolution No. 855 (in force 31 December 2025), amending Decree No. 541 of October 10, 2023, restructured visa-free stay durations: (1) Citizens of 55 countries now receive 30 days within any 60-day period (reduced from prior 60 days within 120-day period); (2) GCC citizens (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman, Bahrain) now receive 180 days within any 360-day period (significantly upgraded); (3) Citizens of Serbia, Turkey, Ukraine, Mongolia: 90 days within 180-day period; (4) Uzbekistan: 60 days within 180-day period. CIS members with bilateral civilian passport agreements (AZE, ARM, GEO, MDA, BLR, KAZ, RUS, TJK) retain indefinite stay rights. E-visa is universally available to all nationalities via evisa.e-gov.kg. Diplomatic and official/service passport holders may have separate bilateral arrangements.

credential.subtype: any valid long-term visa (for more than 3 years)

conditional_access.conditions: Visa-free entry for up to 7 days with the right to re-enter visa-free after 21 days from the date of departure from the Kyrgyz Republic, in the presence of long-term visas (for more than 3 years) of the United States of America, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, or Schengen zone. Applies to citizens of Republic of India and People's Republic of China.

Simplified visa regime - only for tourist entry-exit visas of the Kyrgyz Republic (up to 1 month) for foreign citizens who hold a valid residence permit (resident card) of: UAE, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, State of Kuwait, State of Qatar, Brunei Darussalam, Sultanate of Oman, or Kingdom of Bahrain. Eligible nationalities: Algeria, Egypt, India, Jordan, Lebanon, Morocco, Nepal, Pakistan, Tunisia, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh.

Bilateral visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic and service passports. Duration varies by country (30–90 days). Countries: Hungary, India, Indonesia, Iran, Republic of Korea, Kuwait, Pakistan, Romania, Serbia, Turkmenistan, Morocco, China, Chile, Brazil, Singapore, Laos, Lebanon, UAE, Bahrain, Jordan, Saudi Arabia, Japan. Exact per-country durations not individually published on the MFA page.

Bilateral visa-free regime for holders of diplomatic passports ONLY (not service passport). Duration: up to 90 days for most; Slovakia up to 30 days. Countries: Qatar, Italy, Poland, Estonia, Slovak Republic, Switzerland, France, Lithuania, Greece.

cbi.notes: No official citizenship-by-investment program exists in Kyrgyzstan as of June 2026. A 2020 news report noted Kyrgyzstan was 'mulling' such a program but it was never enacted. No statutory CBI program is confirmed by any official government source.

programs.name: Investment Visa (Type I)
programs.notes: Issued to foreign investors or heads of foreign investment companies contributing to the Kyrgyz economy. Minimum 10 million KGS for visa valid up to 5 years; minimum 20 million KGS for visa valid up to 10 years. Eligible sectors include manufacturing, industry, agriculture, banking, energy, education, medicine, engineering and construction, ICT. Family members (spouse, minor children, dependent parents) may receive F-type family visa. Does not directly confer residency or citizenship. Official application channel is the evisa.e-gov.kg portal.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Status (Visa Type DN)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals working remotely in ICT, software development, data and web services, AI and robotics, media and digital arts, gaming, e-sports, and related fields. Must provide proof of remote contracts and evidence of income. Citizens of eligible nations (both visa-exempt CIS/EAEU members and visa-required nationals may apply).
programs.notes: Status established by Presidential amendments to the Law on External Labor Migration (October 9, 2024); permanent procedure codified by Cabinet of Ministers Resolution No. 241 of April 30, 2025 (in force 15 days after official publication). Initial grant for up to 1 year, renewable annually. DN status holders exempt from compulsory residence registration for up to 60 days; no Kyrgyz work permit required while status is valid. Processing time confirmed at 5 working days per the official evisa.e-gov.kg portal (prior record stated 7 business days - corrected to 5 working days per official source).

agreements: EAEU (Eurasian Economic Union) - free movement of labour among Armenia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Russia
CIS (Commonwealth of Independent States) - bilateral visa-free and simplified entry agreements among CIS members

validation_notes: CORRECTED: max_stay_days for the 55-country unilateral visa-free group (AUS, AUT, BEL, BIH, VAT, GBR, HUN, DEU, NLD, GRC, DNK, ISL, IRL, ESP, ITA, CAN, KOR, LVA, LTU, LIE, LUX, MLT, MCO, NZL, NOR, POL, PRT, SGP, SVK, SVN, USA, FIN, FRA, HRV, CZE, CHE, SWE, EST, BRN, JPN, MNE, AND, ARG, BRA, SMR, CHL, ALB, BGR, VNM, ISR, CYP, MKD, ROU, THA, MEX) changed from 60 to 30. Cabinet of Ministers Resolution No. 855 (in force 31 Dec 2025) reduced the stay from '60 days within any 120-day period' to '30 calendar days within any 60-day period'. Prior record had 60 as the value with a note mentioning 30 days only for some entries (AUS, AUT, GBR, DEU, CAN, USA) - now unified correctly as 30 for all 55.

CORRECTED: max_stay_days for GCC countries (SAU, ARE, QAT, KWT, OMN, BHR) changed from 60/90 to 180. Resolution No. 855 upgraded GCC nationals to 180 days within any 360-day period. Prior record showed SAU=90, ARE=90, QAT=60, OMN=60, BHR=60 - all now corrected to 180.
CORRECTED: VNM (Vietnam) max_stay_days changed from 60 to 30. Vietnam is in the 55-country group governed by Resolution No. 855.
CORRECTED: ISR (Israel) max_stay_days changed from 60 to 30. Israel is in the 55-country group governed by Resolution No. 855.
CORRECTED: MNG (Mongolia), SRB (Serbia), TUR (Turkey), UKR (Ukraine) notes updated to reflect 90 days within 180-day period per Resolution No. 855 (values unchanged at 90 days but context updated).
CORRECTED: UZB (Uzbekistan) notes updated to reflect 60 days within 180-day period per Resolution No. 855 (value unchanged at 60 days but context updated).
CORRECTED: Digital Nomad processing time corrected from '7 business days' to '5 working days' per official evisa.e-gov.kg portal.
CORRECTED: RBI official_url changed from mineconom.gov.kg (which timed out / was unavailable) to evisa.e-gov.kg where the Investment Visa (Type I) is confirmed.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: Conditional access entries for IND/CHN with US/UK/Schengen long-term visas (7 days) remain correct.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: GCC residence permit simplified e-visa regime for 11 nationalities remains correct.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: Diplomatic/service passport bilateral arrangements remain as previously recorded.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: CIS/EAEU bilateral civilian passport agreements (indefinite stay) remain correct.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: Maldives (30 days), HK SAR (30 days), Macao SAR (30 days) confirmed on evisa.e-gov.kg.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: No CBI program exists. No visa on arrival exists (e-visa only).

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Official MFA page (mfa.gov.kg) re-fetched and re-confirmed. The page's rendered HTML still reflects pre-Resolution 855 values (60-day group, lower GCC durations) indicating the MFA website display has not been updated to reflect Resolution No. 855 (in force 31 Dec 2025); however the Resolution itself is confirmed by multiple independent reporting sources (VisasNews, Intermark Relocation, TravelManToday, qazinform.com) and its provisions govern current law. Current record correctly captures the Resolution 855 rules.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: CIS indefinite stays (AZE, ARM, GEO, MDA, BLR, KAZ, RUS, TJK) re-confirmed from the official MFA page re-fetch which explicitly listed these as 'indefinitely' under bilateral civilian passport agreements. Wikipedia and some secondary sources show EAEU members as '90 days within 180' (this refers to the registration-exemption window under EAEU treaty, not the stay limit under bilateral civilian passport agreements). The bilateral civilian passport agreements are the controlling instrument for visa-free stay. No change made - entry preserved.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Wikipedia (Visa policy of Kyrgyzstan) claims visa on arrival for Indonesia, Philippines, South Africa, and Venezuela (30 days each at Manas International Airport). This could NOT be confirmed from any official government source (MFA page and evisa.e-gov.kg both contain no mention of visa on arrival). Per safety rules, this claim is NOT added to the record and is documented as a gap instead.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: Full re-fetch of mfa.gov.kg and evisa.e-gov.kg performed. MFA page continues to render pre-Resolution-855 values (60-day group, lower GCC amounts) - website display still not updated. Resolution No. 855 (31 Dec 2025) is confirmed by multiple independent sources (VisasNews, US State Dept updated Feb 10 2026 confirming 30-day rule for US citizens). VisasNews explicitly confirmed the exact 55-country list and GCC 180-day rule. No factual changes to visa_policy, conditional_access, cbi, rbi, or fast_track entries are warranted. Wikipedia's claim of a 'USD 500 voucher' route for Chinese nationals could NOT be confirmed from official MFA page; not added. Visa on arrival gap remains open - no official source mentions it. Record confirmed accurate as of 2026-06-29.
gaps: The exact count of the 55 affected countries has been cited in secondary sources as '55' but the MFA page lists approximately 57-58 country entries in the unilateral group - the discrepancy may reflect counting micro-states (Andorra, Monaco, San Marino, Liechtenstein, Vatican) differently; not individually resolved from the live page.
Exact investment thresholds are stated in KGS; USD equivalents are approximate and exchange-rate dependent - not quoted verbatim from the official source.

Visa on arrival: Wikipedia (Visa policy of Kyrgyzstan, checked 2026-06-27 and 2026-06-29) claims visa on arrival is available at Manas International Airport for citizens of Indonesia (IDN), Philippines (PHL), South Africa (ZAF), and Venezuela (VEN) - 30 days each. A 2022 traveller blog (pinayhakawati.com) documents a real physical VOA process for PHL passport holders at Manas Airport that was reportedly still valid as of July 2025. However, this could NOT be confirmed from any official government source (mfa.gov.kg and evisa.e-gov.kg both contain no mention of visa on arrival; US State Dept Feb 2026 advisory for Kyrgyzstan does not mention VOA). Claim is NOT added to the record; requires re-verification against an official government document before inclusion.
The complete list of nationalities eligible for the Digital Nomad (DN) visa was not individually enumerated from an official source.
Path to permanent residence via the Investment Visa is not stated in the official evisa.e-gov.kg source.
Agreement dates for bilateral diplomatic/service passport visa waiver agreements are not published on the current MFA page.
Bangladesh's inclusion in the GCC-resident simplified e-visa regime list could not be independently confirmed by a second official source.
Per-country stay durations under the diplomatic/service passport bilateral agreements (varying 30–90 days per country) were not individually resolved from the MFA page.
Iran (15-day rule): Whether Resolution No. 855 affected Iran's 15-day unilateral rule could not be confirmed; retained at 15 days as last seen on MFA page.

The 'unilateral foreign initiative' entries (ATG, BRB, HTI, DMA, NAM, VCT, PHL, TTO) were not individually verified to be current/unchanged under Resolution No. 855 - retained from prior official MFA source.

MFA website display (mfa.gov.kg) appears to still show pre-Resolution 855 values as of 2026-06-27 re-check; the official site has not been updated to reflect the December 31 2025 Resolution in its rendered text. The Resolution itself governs current law and is correctly recorded in this file.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa (Type TS)
visa_types.purpose: Entry for tourism and leisure purposes. Available online via the e-visa portal to nationals of all countries.
visa_types.notes: Fee depends on visa category and period - not individually disclosed on the information page. Processing is typically 5 working days per the official portal. Citizens of 55+ countries enter visa-free for 30 days (Resolution No. 855, in force 31 Dec 2025); all other nationalities must apply via e-visa.

visa_types.name: Business Visa (Type B)
visa_types.purpose: Entry for negotiations, conferences, commercial meetings, and certain medical services of a business nature.
visa_types.notes: Available via online e-visa portal to all nationalities. Fee varies by category; not publicly specified on the information page.

visa_types.name: Special Work Visa SW1 / SW2
visa_types.purpose: Entry for equipment technicians, journalists, and charitable/humanitarian workers engaged in specific short-term work activities.
visa_types.notes: Initial grant up to 60 days, extendable up to 1 year. SW1 and SW2 are sub-categories for different work profiles. Separate from the general work permit system.

visa_types.name: Student Visa (Type S)
visa_types.purpose: Entry for academic study at accredited educational institutions in Kyrgyzstan.
visa_types.notes: Available via e-visa portal. Longer-duration study typically requires in-country extension or a different permit category.

visa_types.name: Digital Nomad Visa (Type DN)
visa_types.purpose: Allows remote workers in ICT, software development, AI, gaming, digital media and related sectors to live and work remotely from Kyrgyzstan without a local work permit.
visa_types.notes: Established by Presidential amendments (October 9, 2024) and codified by Cabinet of Ministers Resolution No. 241 (April 30, 2025). Renewable annually. DN status holders are exempt from compulsory residence registration for up to 60 days. Applicants must show proof of remote contracts and income. No Kyrgyz work permit required while status is valid.

visa_types.name: Investment Visa (Type I)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign investors or heads of foreign investment companies making qualifying capital contributions to the Kyrgyz economy.
visa_types.notes: Minimum investment 10 million KGS for a visa valid up to 5 years; minimum 20 million KGS for a visa valid up to 10 years. Eligible sectors include manufacturing, agriculture, banking, energy, education, medicine, engineering, and ICT. Family members (spouse, minor children, dependent parents) may receive an accompanying FF-type family visa. Does not directly confer permanent residency or citizenship.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa (Type TR)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay entry for passage through Kyrgyz territory en route to a third country.
visa_types.notes: Maximum stay is 3 days. Requires proof of onward travel to a third country.

visa_types.name: Relatives / Family Visit Visa (Type RL)
visa_types.purpose: Entry to visit relatives or for family reunification with a Kyrgyz citizen or legal resident.
visa_types.notes: Available via e-visa portal. Separate Family Member (FF) category exists for accompanying dependants of other visa holders.

visa_types.name: Family Member Visa (Type FF)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to spouses, minor children, and dependent parents accompanying or joining the primary visa holder in Kyrgyzstan.
visa_types.notes: Tied to the primary visa holder's status (e.g., Investment Visa, DN Visa). Validity mirrors the primary holder's visa.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic Visa (Type D)
visa_types.purpose: Entry for heads of foreign states and governments, members of their official delegations, and accredited diplomatic staff.
visa_types.notes: Issued through diplomatic channels, not the public e-visa portal. Holders of diplomatic passports from 30+ countries may also enter visa-free under bilateral agreements.

visa_types.name: Official / Service Visa (Type O)
visa_types.purpose: Entry for foreign state officials and their family members travelling on official government business.
visa_types.notes: Validity ranges from up to 90 days to 1 year depending on assignment. Issued through official channels.

visa_types.name: Driver Visa (Type T)
visa_types.purpose: Entry for drivers engaged in international freight and passenger transport operations.
visa_types.notes: Visa validity up to 6 months; maximum 30 days per individual entry. For international freight/transport drivers.

visa_types.name: Ethnic Kyrgyz Visa / Meken Visa (Type M)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to ethnic Kyrgyz persons residing abroad who wish to visit or return to Kyrgyzstan.
visa_types.notes: 'Meken' (meaning 'homeland') visa category. Applicants must demonstrate ethnic Kyrgyz heritage.

visa_types.name: Religious Visa (Type R)
visa_types.purpose: Entry for missionaries, clergy, and individuals engaged in religious cooperation and charitable religious work.
visa_types.notes: Requires documentation of religious organization affiliation and purpose of visit.

visa_types.name: Sapar Visa (Type J)
visa_types.purpose: Entry for participants in state-endorsed business, sports, and major public events, issued on the basis of a government or institutional petition.
visa_types.notes: Requires a state petition or institutional sponsor. Can be extended ('Sapar' translates roughly to 'journey/trip'). Not available via the standard public e-visa portal.

visa_types.name: Leave Visa (Type L)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals to facilitate legal departure from Kyrgyzstan following a visa violation or overstay.
visa_types.notes: Administrative visa to allow departure. Not for general immigration use.

visa_types.name: Emergency Leave Visa (Type E)
visa_types.purpose: Issued free of charge to foreign nationals requiring emergency departure due to a medical emergency.
visa_types.notes: Issued at no cost for medical emergencies. Administrative/humanitarian category.

https://intermarkrelocation.com/news/immigration/kyrgyzstan-visa-free-stay-shortened/ (2026-06-27, secondary source confirming Resolution 855 stay durations)

https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/international-travel/International-Travel-Country-Information-Pages/Kyrgyzstan.html (2026-06-29, US State Dept, updated Feb 10 2026, confirmed 30-day rule for US citizens)
https://kyrgyzconsuae.org/en/visas/ (2026-06-29, Kyrgyz Consulate General in Dubai, semi-official)
https://www.pinayhakawati.com/kyrgyzstan-visa-on-arrival-philippine-passport/ (2026-06-29, non-official traveller blog, 2022 experience, used for gap research only)
